issue: img element missing width and height attributes, causing CLS
recommendation: Add explicit width and height attributes to the tag so the browser can reserve space before the image loads (e.g. )

issue: img element missing loading="lazy" for below-fold image
recommendation: Add loading="lazy" to defer loading of off-screen images and reduce initial page weight

issue: Image served as PNG without modern format alternatives
recommendation: Use <picture> with WebP/AVIF sources and PNG fallback, or use Next.js <Image> component for automatic format negotiation

issue: No srcset or sizes attributes for responsive images
recommendation: Add srcset and sizes attributes to serve appropriately sized images for different viewports

issue: Dynamic content inserted via setTimeout without reserved space
recommendation: Reserve layout space with min-height or CSS aspect-ratio on the container before async content arrives

issue: Layout thrashing in useEffect: offsetHeight read immediately followed by style.minHeight write
recommendation: Batch DOM reads and writes separately, or use requestAnimationFrame to schedule the style write after the read

issue: List items rendered without key prop, causing inefficient reconciliation
recommendation: Add a stable unique key to each mapped element (e.g. key={item} or a unique id)

issue: Grid container has no explicit min-height or aspect-ratio to hold space while items load
recommendation: Set a CSS min-height or skeleton placeholder on the grid section to prevent layout shift when items appear

issue: Direct DOM manipulation via document.querySelector instead of React ref
recommendation: Replace global DOM query with useRef to avoid unintended side effects and improve predictability

issue: Duplicate id="card-item" when Card is rendered in a list
recommendation: Use unique ids (e.g. based on title or index) to avoid DOM conflicts that can confuse assistive tech and cause unpredictable behavior
Remove console.log statements or gate them behind a development-only flag to avoid minor performance overhead

issue: No fetchpriority attribute to hint browser about image importance
recommendation: Add fetchpriority="low" for below-fold promotional images to let the browser prioritize critical resources first
